title: Moving Bulk Data Under Bandwidth Limits
description:
## 500 TB Over a Slow Link, on a Deadline

A media company must move **500 TB** of archived video from a remote production site into Amazon S3. The site has a **constrained internet link** (roughly 100 Mbps usable), and the data must be in AWS **within a few weeks**. Pushing 500 TB over the available link would take many months. Which approach BEST meets the volume, bandwidth, and deadline constraints?

options.text: Order AWS Snowball Edge devices, copy the data locally, and ship them to AWS for offline import into S3
Deploy AWS DataSync agents and transfer the 500 TB over the existing internet link
Use the S3 CLI with multipart upload to push the data over the internet in parallel
Provision AWS Direct Connect to the site and transfer the data over the dedicated link

explanation:
When **data volume vastly exceeds what the available bandwidth can move within the deadline**, the answer is **offline transfer with the Snow Family**. 500 TB over ~100 Mbps would take on the order of a year; Snowball Edge devices let you copy locally and ship the data, completing the move in weeks regardless of the link. (At multi-petabyte/exabyte scale you'd consider Snowmobile, but Snowball Edge fits 500 TB.)

(b) **DataSync** is excellent for online and ongoing transfers, but it still rides the **same slow internet link** — it can't beat physics; the transfer would take months. (c) **S3 multipart/parallel upload** is also bounded by the 100 Mbps link; parallelism doesn't add bandwidth that isn't there. (d) **Direct Connect** delivers a dedicated, higher-bandwidth link, but **provisioning a cross-connect typically takes weeks to months** and incurs ongoing cost — it doesn't meet a few-weeks deadline for a one-time bulk move, and you'd still need to transfer 500 TB over it. The decision rule: compare transfer time over available bandwidth against the deadline; if shipping is faster, choose **Snow (offline)**.

hints.title: Do the math
hints.body: Estimate transfer time = data size / usable bandwidth. If that exceeds the deadline, online transfer is off the table.

hints.title: Online vs. offline
hints.body: DataSync = online over the network; Snow Family = offline physical shipping for petabyte-scale or bandwidth-constrained moves.
